content.thinking: Let me start by reading the relevant files to understand the current state of the codebase before making changes.

Now I have a clear picture of what needs to be changed:

1. **FRG-257**: Remove `border-bottom: 1.5px solid #111` from the `::before` pseudo-elements for section headers (lines 84-101 in resume-styles.css)

2. **FRG-258**: Improve PDF styles - better spacing, visual hierarchy, polished print output. The current design uses those harsh lines as separators; we need something more subtle.

My plan:
1. Remove the `border-bottom` from `::before` pseudo-elements
2. Replace with a more subtle separator - perhaps a lighter color (`#e5e7eb` or `#ddd`) or just better spacing


3. Refine the overall visual hierarchy and spacing throughout, increase section breathing room, and improve the print styles with better letter spacing and cleaner print rules.

For the improved design, I'll remove those harsh dark borders and replace them with either very subtle light gray lines or rely on spacing alone to separate sections. I'll also boost the font weights on headers, improve the spacing between roles, and add page-break-avoid rules to keep content together when printing.

Still writing CSS styles... Updating the CSS styles... I'm going back and forth on whether to remove the borders entirely or keep them subtle. The task mentions PDF output looks bad with harsh lines, but also suggests lighter colors as an alternative. I think a very light gray border like `#e2e8f0` strikes the right balance—it's still a visual separator but won't render harshly in PDFs. I'll also increase spacing between sections and roles, remove box shadows for print, and add page-break-inside properties to improve the overall layout. Writing the updated CSS file...

input.old_string:
/**
 * Resume / Cover Letter Render Styles — modern.css spec
 *
 * Styles the custom XML elements produced by the AI pipeline:
 *   <document>, <header>, <name>, <contact>, <experience>, etc.
 *
 * These custom elements are rendered as HTML via DOMPurify + v-html.
 * The browser treats unknown elements as inline by default, so each
 * element that needs block-level layout must have display set explicitly.
 *
 * Design spec:
 *   Font:      Inter, 'DM Sans', system-ui, -apple-system, sans-serif
 *   Name:      22px / 700
 *   Sections:  13px / 600 / ALL CAPS / letter-spacing 0.05em / border-bottom 1.5px solid
 *   Body:      13px / 400 / line-height 1.5
 *   Contact:   12px / #555
 *   Layout:    max-width 800px, padding 48px 64px
 */

input.command:
git add components/resume-styles.css && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix: remove harsh section header lines and polish PDF styles (FRG-257, FRG-258)

FRG-257: Replace bold 1.5px solid #111 border-bottom on section ::before
labels with a subtle 1px solid #e2e8f0 separator and softer color (#444).
Also reduces font size to 11px and increases letter-spacing for cleaner
typographic hierarchy.

FRG-258: Overall visual polish improvements:
- Add subtle border-bottom separator under the header block
- Letter format: remove header border (inappropriate for letters)
- Increase section and role vertical spacing for breathing room
- Increase paragraph line-height (1.6 → 1.7) and margin-bottom (0.85 → 1rem)
- Add 3rem top margin on signature for more formal letter layout
- Print: strip box-shadow/border-radius from document wrapper
- Print: use slightly darker separator color (#d1d5db) for better contrast on paper
- Print: add page-break-inside:avoid for summary, experience, and skills sections

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

input.command:
gh pr create --title "fix: remove harsh section header lines and polish PDF styles (FRG-257, FRG-258)" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary

- **FRG-257**: Replaces the harsh `1.5px solid #111` border-bottom on resume section headers with a subtle `1px solid #e2e8f0` separator. Section label text color softened to `#444`, font-size reduced to `11px`, letter-spacing increased to `0.08em` — cleaner typographic hierarchy without a harsh rule.
- **FRG-258**: Visual polish pass for resume and letter format, targeting both screen preview and PDF/print output.

## Changes in `components/resume-styles.css`

| Area | Before | After |
|------|--------|-------|
| Section header line | `1.5px solid #111` (dark, harsh) | `1px solid #e2e8f0` (subtle gray) |
| Section header color | `#111` | `#444` |
| Header block | no separator | subtle `1px solid #e2e8f0` bottom border + padding |
| Letter header | same border as resume | border removed (not appropriate for letters) |
| Section `margin-bottom` | `1.25rem` | `1.5rem` |
| Role `margin-bottom` | `1rem` | `1.1rem` |
| Paragraph `line-height` | `1.6` | `1.7` |
| Paragraph `margin-bottom` | `0.85rem` | `1rem` |
| Letter date color | `#111` | `#555` |
| Signature `margin-top` | `2.5rem` | `3rem` |
| Print: document `box-shadow` | present | removed |
| Print: document `border-radius` | present | removed |
| Print: additional `break-inside:avoid` | role, education | + summary, experience, skills |

## Test plan

- [ ] Load resume sample — verify section headers show subtle gray line instead of bold black line
- [ ] Load letter sample — verify header block has no separator line, spacing looks correct
- [ ] Print/export to PDF from browser — confirm no box-shadow artifact, clean separator colors
- [ ] Check both formats at different zoom levels
- [ ] Verify mobile responsive styles still apply correctly

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
EOF
)"

content.text:
## Status
DONE

## What Changed
- `components/resume-styles.css` — replaced harsh `1.5px solid #111` section header borders with a subtle `1px solid #e2e8f0` gray; softened label color to `#444`, reduced font-size to `11px`, increased letter-spacing. Added a subtle bottom border to the header block (and removed it for cover letters). Increased section/role/paragraph spacing. Improved `@media print` to remove `box-shadow`/`border-radius` from the document wrapper and add `break-inside: avoid` to more sections.

## Key Decisions
- Kept a separator line but made it `1px solid #e2e8f0` (slate-200) rather than removing it entirely — provides visual structure without looking harsh in print.
- Cover letter header gets `border-bottom: none` since a ruled line under the sender's address doesn't suit the letter format.
- Print uses `#d1d5db` (slightly darker) for separators so they remain visible on paper where light grays can disappear.

## PR / Branch
- Branch: `fix/frg-257-258-resume-pdf-styles`
- PR: https://github.com/kimwwk/ohmydoc-using-claude-code-agent/pull/41

## Status
DONE

## What Changed
- `components/resume-styles.css` — replaced harsh `1.5px solid #111` section header borders with a subtle `1px solid #e2e8f0` gray; softened label color to `#444`, reduced font-size to `11px`, increased letter-spacing. Added a subtle bottom border to the header block (and removed it for cover letters). Increased section/role/paragraph spacing. Improved `@media print` to remove `box-shadow`/`border-radius` from the document wrapper and add `break-inside: avoid` to more sections.

## Key Decisions
- Kept a separator line but made it `1px solid #e2e8f0` (slate-200) rather than removing it entirely — provides visual structure without looking harsh in print.
- Cover letter header gets `border-bottom: none` since a ruled line under the sender's address doesn't suit the letter format.
- Print uses `#d1d5db` (slightly darker) for separators so they remain visible on paper where light grays can disappear.

## PR / Branch
